channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump names latest Cabinet picks as Jan. 20 inauguration nears
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Christine Toretti as ambassador to Sweden and Leo Terrell as senior counsel to the assistant attorney general for civil rights in the Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Handshakes of Old Partners
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Special Counsel Weiss expected to release Hunter Biden report as soon as next week
item.description: First son Hunter Biden was found guilty, but was pardoned by his father, President Joe Biden, in the felony firearm offense
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic Sen. John Fetterman to meet with Trump at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman will become the chamber's first Democrat to meet with President-elect Donald Trump since the election and plans to travel to Trump's private Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Laken Riley Act overwhelmingly clears first hurdle in Senate with help from Democrats
item.description: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the Laken Riley Act, a bill aimed at curbing crime by migrants, in the first vote of the year by the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA suburb doubles down on sanctuary city policies as neighboring city burns
item.description: As whole neighborhoods in Los Angeles burn to the ground, city council members in nearby Long Beach, California, voted to double down on their sanctuary city policies, vowing to resist President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Emhoff appear to ignore Trump, Obama as outgoing veep grimaces at presidential banter
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and President-elect Donald Trump apparently did not acknowledge each other at the Washington National Cathedral during Jimmy Carter's state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Pence shake hands at Carter funeral in first public meeting since leaving office
item.description: President-elect Trump shook hands with his former vice president, Mike Pence, at Jimmy Carter's funeral but did not shake hands with former second lady Karen Pence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans introduce bill to repurchase Panama Canal after Trump raises concerns of Chinese control
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson, R-S.D., introduced the Panama Canal Repurchase Act, co-sponsored by 15 other House Republicans, amid President-elect Trump's concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes ICC sanctions bill; GOP Senate leader promises to bring it to floor
item.description: The House on Thursday passed legislation to sanction officials with the International Criminal Court (ICC), pushing back against the court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister for their actions during Israel’s war against H
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some notable quotes from Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Jimmy Carter was remembered at his funeral Thursday at Washington National Cathedral as a man of “character” and “something of a miracle” with “prophetic” vision.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US orange juice and steel will be on Canada's list for retaliation if Trump imposes tariffs
item.description: A senior official says Canada is looking at putting retaliatory tariffs on American orange juice, sinks, toilets and some steel products if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump goes ahead with his threat to impose 25% on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden eulogizes ‘great American’ Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Thursday eulogized former President Carter, hailing the 39th president as a man of character who left an enduring legacy both during his time in the White House and after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After his only child was killed on 9/11, Ken Fairben looked for justice in a far-off military courtroom on the Guantánamo Bay naval base in Cuba. He traveled there multiple times to observe hearings for accused Sept.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis asks Georgia’s top court to review decision booting her from Trump case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis (D) has asked Georgia’s top court to review her disqualification from the election subversion case against President-elect Trump and several allies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In one of the most important cases of the social media age, free speech and national security collide at the Supreme Court on Friday in arguments over the fate of TikTok, a wildly popular digital platform that roughly half the people in the United States use for entertainment and i
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden cancels trip to Italy, meant as final foreign visit of presidency, as fires rage in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden canceled the final overseas trip of his presidency just hours before he was set to depart for Rome and the Vatican, choosing to remain in Washington to monitor the response to devastating fires raging in California.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats warn party must look beyond 'blue wall' to win
item.description: When Democrats lost the White House in November and watched the Senate flip and the House remain in Republican control, they immediately set their sights on the next fight in 2028. But some Democrats say that's a mistake.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Garland to release Smith's Jan. 6 report, hold back Mar-a-Lago report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland plans to release only the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s plans to subvert the transfer of power after his loss in the 2020 election, holding back on sharing the Mar-a-Lago report while the president-elect's two co-defendants
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: In a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump would not rule out using military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, citing national security interests.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump’s latest attempt to get Friday’s hush money sentencing called off
item.description: In a one-sentence ruling following an emergency hearing, Judge Ellen Gesmer denied Trump’s request for an immediate order that would spare him from being sentenced while he appeals Judge Juan M. Merchan’s decision last week to uphold the historic verdict.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: U.K. leader Starmer slams ‘lies and misinformation’ after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned "lies and misinformation" that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Fight over Florida tuition for undocumented immigrants continues years later
item.description: A Florida senator wants to repeal a law that grants in-state tuition rates to undocumented immigrants. State Senator Randy Fine says he just wants to put Floridians first, but the bill is facing backlash from across the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
